input.headline: NSW school endures floods, fires and an angry camel to mark 180 years

raw.headline: NSW school endures floods, fires and an angry camel to mark 180 years

winner: The school's principal said the floods, fires, and angry camel were just minor inconveniences—the real tragedy was running out of "survived another year" stickers for the awards ceremony.

candidates.text: The principal tried explaining the school's rough year as part of history—floods, fires, and that camel incident—but the kids kept interrupting with, "Wait, is the camel in our curriculum?
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with the camel in curriculum, concise and specific joke that ties back to the headline"}

candidates.text: Why did the camel refuse to leave the flooded NSW school? It said, "I'm not stepping into this mess, I just got my hooves waxed!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific to the headline, but the humor is weak and relies on a forced pun that doesn't fully land."}

candidates.text: The principal said it was their worst year yet—floods, fires, and a rogue camel—but at least they didn’t have to deal with any *dromedary drama*.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative use of 'dromedary drama' adds a humorous twist, aligns well with the headline, and is concise and surprising."}

candidates.text: The school's principal said the floods, fires, and angry camel were just minor inconveniences—the real tragedy was running out of "survived another year" stickers for the awards ceremony.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with the stickers, specific to the school's situation, concise and funny."}

candidates.text: The school's 180th anniversary was so uneventful that even the camel refused to make a scene—it just stood there, judging everyone silently.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific, but the humor falls flat due to a lack of clear connection between the camel and the school's anniversary. The 'judging everyone silently' line feels forced and doesn't fully align with the headline's dramatic elements."}
